name: Momos (Tibetan Steamed Dumplings)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Daikon, spinach or cabbage, chopped fine
2 Green onions, chopped (white and green parts only)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour with water; knead until a smooth dough forms.
2. Cover the dough and let it rest for 30 minutes.
3. Bring a large pot of water to a boil.
4. Divide the dough into 12-18 pieces and roll each piece into small, flat circles.
5. In a bowl, mash together all filling ingredients: ground beef (or chicken), onion, daikon, ginger, garlic, cilantro, salt, and green onions.
6. Place a spoonful of filling on each dough circle, fold the dough over to seal, and crimp the edges.
7. Place the dumplings in a steamer and steam on high for 30 minutes.
8. Serve with a mild tomato salsa ("Tsal") made from chopped tomatoes, cilantro, green onions, and garlic, or with Sriracha sauce and/or soy sauce.
Recipe from Jigme Topgyal, a Tibetan. In his native Tibet, these would be made using roasted barley flour called "Tsampa". The alternate filling typically consists of ground chicken mashed with onion, daikon, fresh ginger, garlic, and cilantro. A vegetarian filling often includes chopped cabbage, bok choy, tofu, green onions, ginger, and garlic.
